topic: Proximal bridge quotient identification without completeness

rejection.reason: R1 fires because the proposed quotient criterion is already a named published result rather than an open problem: Zhang, Li, Miao, and Tchetgen Tchetgen state that, when the outcome bridge equation holds, a linear functional of a possibly nonunique bridge is identified iff the functional's loading lies in the orthocomplement of the bridge operator nullspace. In finite negative-control matrices this is exactly the requested row-space/null-annihilator certificate; the requested 3x3 witness would be an illustration of the published criterion, not a new identification theorem.

evidence_refs.claim: Proposition 2.3: under the outcome bridge equation, beta_o is identified iff phi_o is in N(T_o)^perp; the paper explicitly studies ATE inference without unique bridge solutions.
evidence_refs.author: Zhang, Li, Miao, Tchetgen Tchetgen

evidence_refs.claim: The arXiv version states that only bridge existence is required for ATE identification and bridge functions need not be unique.
evidence_refs.author: Zhang, Li, Miao, Tchetgen Tchetgen

evidence_refs.excerpt: finite null-annihilator / row-space criterion for proximal ATE invariance

suggested_directions: Re-run on a genuinely new finite-observed-law construction problem: classify minimal 2x2 or 3x3 negative-control witnesses that realize each phase of unique bridge, nonunique-but-quotient-identified ATE, and non-singleton ATE.

Re-run on sharp partial identification without completeness: characterize when bridge-null directions are induced by actual latent negative-control causal models rather than merely by algebraic bridge fibers.
Re-run on inference for estimated quotient certificates: derive fixed-rank asymptotic behavior and diagnostics for near-nullspace violations of the Zhang et al. null-annihilator condition.

literature_map:
1. Miao, Geng, and Tchetgen Tchetgen (2018, Biometrika) introduced proximal/proxy identification through outcome bridge functions and completeness-style relevance conditions.
2. Cui, Pu, Shi, Miao, and Tchetgen Tchetgen (2024, JASA) developed semiparametric proximal ATE/ATT theory and proximal doubly robust estimators.
3. Tchetgen Tchetgen et al. and longitudinal proximal work extend bridge identification to time-varying settings, again using Fredholm bridge equations and completeness/relevance conditions.
4. Zhang, Li, Miao, and Tchetgen Tchetgen (2023, Statistics & Probability Letters) is the decisive anchor for this topic: it studies proximal inference without unique bridge solutions.
5. In that paper, Proposition 2.3 gives the exact nullspace criterion: a bridge linear functional is identified iff its loading annihilates the nullspace of the outcome bridge operator.
6. The same paper applies this to counterfactual means and ATE/ATT and constructs regular root-n estimators under additional assumptions.
7. Ghassami, Shpitser, and Tchetgen Tchetgen (2024, arXiv) study partial identification with proxy variables when completeness fails and bridge/effect point identification is unavailable in general.
8. This creates a nearby open direction around the boundary between partial-ID intervals and quotient singleton cases, but the null-annihilator singleton criterion itself is not open.
9. The finite matrix version requested by the topic is the finite-dimensional specialization of N(T_o)^perp, equivalently target row-space membership or zero contrast on every bridge null vector.
10. A 3x3 hand witness would be useful pedagogically, but by itself it would instantiate an already-published theorem rather than establish a new identification result.
prior_proposal_map:
1. Active qid eid_proximal_bridge_quotient_v1 has only init/state files; no active proposal content was available to mine.
2. The closest banked entry is eid_proximal_phase_v1, which already framed null-annihilator proximal ATE identification but was downgraded.
3. eid_proximal_completeness_v1 sharpened the same issue: nonunique bridges can still identify ATE, but reviewers marked the row-space fact as known.
4. Reviewer JSONs around eid_proximal_completeness_v1 explicitly flagged the finite null-annihilator/row-space criterion for proximal ATE invariance.
5. Reviewer pressure across proximal runs asks for concrete finite observed laws, explicit matrices, and endpoint/nullspace certificates.
6. The local catalogue therefore supports rejection of the abstract quotient theorem as a new target, while preserving finite witness construction as a possible adjacent exercise.
7. Adjacent eid_proximal_did_bridge_frontier_v1 confirms the finite witness demand and warns against merely specializing generic proximal completeness.
8. pid_mediation_proxy_v1 is useful mainly as a caution that full-fiber realizability cannot be assumed when moving from quotient point ID to sharp partial ID.
